sanskrit:
अहङ्कारं बलं दर्पं कामं क्रोधं परिग्रहम्।
विमुच्य निर्ममः शान्तो ब्रह्मभूयाय कल्पते॥
transliteration:
ahaṅkāraṃ balaṃ darpaṃ kāmaṃ krodhaṃ parigraham |
vimucya nirmamaḥ śānto brahma-bhūyāya kalpate ||
literal_meaning: Having released false-identity, brute-force, arrogance, craving, anger and hoarding; free from ‘mine’, tranquil—one becomes fit for Brahman-realization.
contextual_meaning: The psyche that has systematically dismantled its defensive armoring (ego inflation, power drives, pride, lust, rage, possessiveness) enters a low-arousal, non-clinging state in which the default mode network quiets and the self-referential narrative dissolves, making trans-personal awareness possible.
psychological_state: Post-deconstructive stillness: the mind is no longer hijacked by self-enhancement or threat-defense loops; identity has shifted from narrative-self to open awareness.
trigger_scenario: After a burnout-induced sabbatical you donate half your possessions, delete social media, and notice the compulsive inner narrator has fallen silent; for the first time the afternoon light feels unconditionally adequate.
anchor_text: Drop the story of 'I, me, mine' and rest in quiet openness.
